perturbation.original_text: GSEnergy agrees to maintain all Information in secrecy and confidence for a period of seven (7) years from the date of disclosure thereof, using the same degree of care as is used to protect its own Information. In no event will this degree of care be less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: GSEnergy agrees to maintain all Information in secrecy and confidence using a commercially reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific 'seven (7) years' timeframe for maintaining confidentiality, the agreement becomes ambiguous about the duration of this obligation. This contradicts Section 1 which defines Information that GSEnergy should maintain as confidential. The change introduces uncertainty and potential disputes regarding the length of the confidentiality period because GSEnergy is expected to maintain all Information mentioned in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with the Prospective Purchase, the Company will provide GSEnergy with certain proprietary and commercially sensitive information and develop further such information with GSEnergy (all of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as "Information") which it wishes GSEnergy to maintain as confidential. GSEnergy agrees that it will safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 7-year confidentiality period. The changed text removes this timeframe, making the duration of confidentiality ambiguous. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that GSEnergy must maintain certain 'Information' as confidential. Without a defined timeframe, it's unclear how long GSEnergy is obligated to maintain this confidentiality, creating a conflict and uncertainty about the enforceability of the confidentiality obligation regarding the 'Information'.

perturbation.original_text: If GSEnergy desires to use individuals not employed by GSEnergy, GSEnergy shall not disclose Information to these individuals without obtaining prior written approval from the Company and having appropriate non-disclosure agreements signed in form and substance satisfactory to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: If GSEnergy desires to use individuals not employed by GSEnergy, GSEnergy shall disclose Information to these individuals.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for prior written approval and NDA’s, Section 3 now directly contradicts the core purpose of the agreement. It now allows the unrestricted disclosure of 'Information' to non-employees, nullifying the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 1. The change makes GSEnergy liable to any breach of security from the individuals not employed by them, rendering Section 1 unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with the Prospective Purchase, the Company will provide GSEnergy with certain proprietary and commercially sensitive information and develop further such information with GSEnergy (all of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as "Information") which it wishes GSEnergy to maintain as confidential. GSEnergy agrees that it will safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis.
perturbation.justification: The original text required GSEnergy to obtain prior written approval and NDAs before disclosing 'Information' to non-employees, thus protecting the Company's confidential information. The changed text removes this requirement, now allowing GSEnergy to freely disclose 'Information' to non-employees. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that GSEnergy agrees to safeguard such 'Information' and maintain its confidentiality. The change effectively nullifies the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction and making the agreement unenforceable with respect to confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: It will not constitute a violation of this Agreement for GSEnergy to disclose Information as required by a governmental body or a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law, provided that the Company has been given notice of such requirement and been afforded a reasonable opportunity to contest it, if applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: It will not constitute a violation of this Agreement for GSEnergy to disclose Information as required by a governmental body or a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'provided that the Company has been given notice of such requirement and been afforded a reasonable opportunity to contest it, if applicable,' from Section 4, GSEnergy is no longer required to notify the Company before disclosing confidential information under legal compulsion. This undermines the Company's ability to protect its confidential information as outlined in Section 1, since they have no way to control that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with the Prospective Purchase, the Company will provide GSEnergy with certain proprietary and commercially sensitive information and develop further such information with GSEnergy (all of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as "Information") which it wishes GSEnergy to maintain as confidential. GSEnergy agrees that it will safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis.
perturbation.justification: The original text required GSEnergy to notify the Company and provide an opportunity to contest disclosure of Information. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states that GSEnergy will safeguard Information and maintain its confidentiality. By removing the notification requirement, GSEnergy can disclose confidential information without the Company's knowledge or ability to contest it, directly contradicting the obligation to safeguard and maintain confidentiality, creating a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: If any provision of this Agreement is or becomes or is deemed invalid, illegal or unenforceable in any jurisdiction, such provision shall be deemed amended to conform to applicable laws so as to be valid and enforceable or, if it cannot be so amended without materially altering the intention of the parties, it shall be stricken, and the remainder of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: If any provision of this Agreement is or becomes or is deemed invalid, illegal or unenforceable in any jurisdiction, such provision shall be stricken, and the remainder of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the language 'such provision shall be deemed amended to conform to applicable laws so as to be valid and enforceable or,' from Section 5, if a provision is deemed invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, it will automatically be stricken. This conflicts with the overall intention of the agreement where the provision aims for agreement validity. With this change, if Section 2 (confidentiality period) were challenged, it would be immediately removed, negating the confidentiality mentioned in Section 1, instead of attempting to be amended to be valid, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with the Prospective Purchase, the Company will provide GSEnergy with certain proprietary and commercially sensitive information and develop further such information with GSEnergy (all of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as "Information") which it wishes GSEnergy to maintain as confidential. GSEnergy agrees that it will safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for a provision deemed invalid to be amended to conform to applicable laws. The changed text removes this option, mandating that the provision be stricken. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which discusses maintaining confidentiality. If the provision regarding the confidentiality period (Section 2, as alluded to in the Perturbation Explanation) is challenged, the changed text dictates it be immediately removed, negating the confidentiality agreement detailed in Section 1. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the agreement's intention to maintain confidentiality is undermined by the removal of the amendment option.

perturbation.original_text: All notices from GSEnergy hereunder shall be in writing and delivered to the attention of Mr. Michael J. Hager, New England Power Company, 25 Research Drive, Westborough, MA 01582. All notices from the Company hereunder shall be in writing and delivered to GSEnergy at the above address.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices from the Company hereunder shall be in writing and delivered to GSEnergy at the above address.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'All notices from GSEnergy hereunder shall be in writing and delivered to the attention of Mr. Michael J. Hager, New England Power Company, 25 Research Drive, Westborough, MA 01582', from Section 8, GSEnergy has no requirement for notice. This negates Section 4 where GSEnergy needs to give notice to the company for disclosure that is required by a governmental body. Now the company is not in the loop, which contradicts the confidentiality of section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with the Prospective Purchase, the Company will provide GSEnergy with certain proprietary and commercially sensitive information and develop further such information with GSEnergy (all of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as "Information") which it wishes GSEnergy to maintain as confidential. GSEnergy agrees that it will safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis.
perturbation.justification: The original text required GSEnergy to provide notices to a specific address. The changed text removes this requirement, contradicting the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Company will provide GSEnergy with confidential information and that GSEnergy will maintain it as confidential. Without the notice requirement, GSEnergy is no longer obligated to notify the Company regarding disclosures, creating a strong contradiction that compromises confidentiality.
